strengths: Proposes a highly novel and physically interpretable framework (Unified Dual-Mask Physical Model) that successfully shifts away from standard Lambertian assumptions.
Provides a clear and logical narrative progression, effectively identifying the limitations of EPI viewpoint consistency in modern deep learning architectures.
Displays solid mathematical formalization in the methodology, specifically defining the 4D cost volume formulation and architectural progression.
weaknesses: Contains fewer than 30 citations, demonstrating an insufficient review of related literature for a TCSVT submission.
Lacks essential visual evidence of actual figures (relying on textual placeholders), standard BadPix metrics, and detailed ablation studies, severely limiting experimental validation.
The proposed model's actual performance on non-Lambertian scenes (MAE 0.390) is only marginally better than the baseline failures it critiques (MAE 0.411), undermining the claimed paradigm shift.
Document formatting fails IEEE standards due to the presence of Markdown residuals (e.g., **, ##) instead of proper LaTeX environments.
key_gaps_vs_tcsvt: Format Non-Compliance: The manuscript contains Markdown residuals and fails to demonstrate standard IEEEtran class formatting, markboth headers, and proper equation environments.
Insufficient Literature Context: With under 30 references, the paper lacks the comprehensive scholarly grounding expected in IEEE TCSVT, particularly missing recent state-of-the-art comparisons.

Experimental Rigor and Visuals: The paper lacks actual visual figures (only TikZ/text placeholders are implied), standard light field metrics like BadPix, and quantitative ablation studies required to validate the physical dual-mask model.
